title: Solenostemon silvaticus
taxonomy: Solenostemon silvaticus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Solenostemon, which is a group of plants commonly known as coleus. The plant is native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The classification of Solenostemon silvaticus is further broken down into its botanical name, which is a combination of its genus and species. As a member of the Lamiaceae family, Solenostemon silvaticus is closely related to other plants in the mint family.

intro: Solenostemon silvaticus, also known as Coleus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Africa and widely cultivated for its ornamental value. The leaves of Solenostemon silvaticus have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
